Action Plan Name: Remediation of Cashapp’s External (CFBP) Complaints SLA 60-day breach
Action Plan Description: The action plan is designed to restore compliance with the 60-day CFPB SLA by addressing backlog and ensuring sustained stabilization.
Action Plan Validation Steps:
Action 1: Implement the “Hold the Line” Strategy
 Establish a cutoff date of September 10th, beyond which all new complaint inflow will be protected.
 Redirect the majority of staff to focus forward, ensuring new inflow is resolved within SLA and building a 9-day protective buffer.
 Deploy a smaller, dedicated backlog crew to work backward and steadily reduce overdue exposure without compromising forward protection.
 

 Action 2: Maintain a Forward Protection Team
 Create a dedicated ‘Hold the Line’ team assigned exclusively to complaints due after the September 10th cutoff.
 This team will focus solely on maintaining a forward buffer so that new inflow never falls out of SLA again.
 

 Action 3: Drive Backlog Reduction
 Assign a weekday backlog team dedicated to addressing cases older than the cutoff date.
 Supplement efforts with voluntary overtime and weekend shifts to accelerate backlog reduction.
 Apply additional support from supplemental staffing as resources become available, ensuring steady progress until the backlog is eliminated.
Closure Request Justification:
All elements of the action plan have either been successfully executed or are in a stable, controlled trajectory with continued improvement. Specifically:
 

 SLA performance has exceeded the original 60-day goal and is on pace to meet more aggressive 45- day target.
 

 Backlog reduction has been steady and outpaced inflow for multiple consecutive weeks.
 

 Staffing and training investments (20 individuals) have begun yielding measurable throughput improvements.
 

 Forward protection buffers have been preserved, preventing SLA breaches for new inflow.
 

 Known risks have been mitigated or are actively managed with minimal impact on core metrics.
 

 Given these results, the conditions set forth in the action plan have been satisfied. The strategy has not only stabilized complaint operations but also positioned the team for long-term performance gains. Closure of this action plan is therefore recommended.
Closure Validation Justification: All Action Plan items have been met or exceed the goal.

Action Plan Name: Cashapp’s External (CFBP) Complaints SLA 15 day Compliance
Action Plan Description: Scaled workforce capacity that ensures SLA compliance even under higher complaint volumes.
Action Plan Validation Steps:
Action 1: Uplift and Streamline Complaint Handling (Single Complaint Handler Model)
 Implement the Single Complaint Handler (SCH) model to reduce redundancy and improve accountability.
 Streamline workflows to ensure individual ownership and faster resolution.
 Update and enhance documentation to improve usability and enable consistent execution.
 Intended Outcome: Increased handler efficiency, reduced cycle times, and improved overall SLA compliance.
 

 Action 2: Expedite Responses for Low-Risk Complaint Populations
 Identify complaint populations eligible for standardized templated responses with a high confidence rate.
 Launch a 90-day pilot beginning August 20, 2025, with approved low-risk categories.
 Monitor outcomes and evaluate effectiveness for sustained efficiency gains.
 Expand implementation across all complaint intake channels following pilot validation.
 Intended Outcome: Faster resolution of low-risk cases, creating additional capacity for complex complaints.
 

 Action 3: Increase CCOT Capacity
 Hire 20 additional complaint handlers during Q3–Q4 2025.
 Ensure new hires are onboarded, trained, and at full productivity by year-end.
 Adjust staffing models proactively to hedge against future complaint volume increases.
 

 Intended Outcome: Scaled workforce capacity that ensures SLA compliance even under higher complaint volumes.
Progress Updates: Action Plan Validation Date to be 04/15/2026

Action Plan Name: Risk Accept or Develop Process for Self-Service Disputes to Stop Collection Activities
Issue Source: Second Line of Defense (2LoD) Review of First Line of Defense (1LoD) Activities
Action Plan Description:
Collections will conduct data analysis to determine how many customer in Collections raise disputes utilising self-service function and assess for Risk Acceptance. If Risk Acceptance is needed, a request will be submitted; if not, a new process will be developed. 
 This new process will involve engaging the Data Science team to build a daily report tracking North American customers in collections who raise disputes via the self-service portal. Operational Support will then implement procedures to act on this daily report, leveraging the existing Customer Service "Billing Dispute Overview" process framework.

 2. If Collections does not risk accept, sight the daily report of NA customer in collections who raise disputes. Sample test to ensure that DCAs have been notified to stop collection activities during the dispute period.

Action Plan Description:
Problem: Managed merchants remain in an “Escalated to MLRO” state without timely decision or completion, causing long-overdue periodic reviews and heightened residual AML/CTF risk.
 Root causes:
 No hard SLA or auto-enforcement for RM and MLRO escalations (status can persist indefinitely).
 Insufficient gating in tooling, for managed merchants.
 Lack of WIP limits for MLRO queue; manual follow-ups only.
Action Plan Validation Steps:
Fix the process design flaw that allows merchants to remain indefinitely “escalated to MLRO” without periodic review completion.
 Clear the backlog of overdue periodic review cases efficiently and diligently, prioritizing highest risk.
 Propose an automated evergreen solution, with associated SLAs and escalations into tooling and governance so this does not recur.

Action Plan Name: Removing Late Capture check from non-fraud workflows
Action Plan Description: The remediation strategy is to remove the Late Capture step (and any related 10.3 error instructions) from the Goods/Services Not Received, Goods/Services Not as Described, Refund Credit Not Processed, Paid by Other Means, and Cancel Subscriptions authorized workflows where the step is no longer required to prevent filing errors. This will be accomplished through a formal PCR that will be reviewed by relevant stakeholders in the Risk Ops, Compliance, Product, Legal, and ML business areas. Once the PCR is approved, recorded changes will be made to the relevant Knowledge Articles for Dispute Ops, and the Late Capture logic will be removed from CIF. Success criteria will be determined by Dispute Ops as they monitor the volume of filed claims and review for mishandled cases related to Late Captures.
Action Plan Validation Steps:
- Review PCR approval matrix
 - Confirm approvals from Content & Compliance on KA changes
 - Review report that confirms/evidences removal of logic in CIF
 - Evidence that process update email was sent to all impacted internal business areas 
 - Confirm internal comms draft approval by Disputes leadership

Action Plan Description: Formalize and document an Internal Audit Manual in accordance with the Institute of Internal Auditors (IIA) Global Internal Audit Standards. This manual will outline the internal audit methodology across the entire audit lifecycle, encompassing: Audit Execution Standards: - Risk assessment - Planning and scoping - Sampling techniques - Design assessment and operating effectiveness testing approach - Audit deliverable review procedures and documentation standards, including internal oversight for third-party co-source vendors - Issue and reporting rating metrics - Remediation and validation methodology - Escalation procedures including when audits and/or remediation timelines are deferred. Additionally, the Head of Internal Audit will conduct an annual resource capacity model assessment (typically in Q4) to support the execution of the upcoming annual internal audit plan. This assessment will identify resource needs, including internal technical gaps, and propose solutions such as hiring new resources or supplementing the existing team with third-party co-source teams for subject matter expertise and audit execution.

Action Plan Linked To: Issue Management | Issue Management Program | ID 1022601
Closure Request Justification:
PR Checklist and review has been updated. Our code review process now mandates confirmation that the new changes are mapped in both code and tests. Eng also has github actions warning of changes within forms.
 We've established larger testing Parties (example within visa changes https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QnBjQAze78tZ7EJROGYRGG2YSQhUFjeodeNbCYZw6ew/edit?gid=122173944#gid=122173944 and $pay forms https://www.notion.so/cashappcash/Pay-in-app-Disputes-testing-party-25b54e8e10318036b6bfcb916944595b) where we check with figma to ensure that the question ids are properly mapped and tested and all comms are properly sent.
 Recordings are also provided that allow further reference for future troubleshooting.
 Automated snapshot testing for forms (regression protection) has been added. https://github.com/squareup/cash-disputron/pull/8027
 We require the creation and maintenance of comprehensive snapshot tests for all forms. This process captures all permutation and branching to ensure that branches aren't changed unintentionally. Question text is also validated within this update. This is to ensure a stable baseline and identifying deviations.
 Monitoring: from data side, we are verifying in snowflake that the questions have been hit in staging and in prod.
 

 All of this ensures that no branch (optionality is fully tested, validated, and monitored)
Closure Validation Justification: Testing materials and updated code review process adequately mitigate this risk and significantly decreases the chance of a reoccurrence.

Action Plan Name: CS and Loan Ops will collaborate on reviewing procedures and Square Wise articles
Issue Source: Second Line of Defense (2LoD) Review of First Line of Defense (1LoD) Activities
Action Plan Description:
CS and Loan Ops will collaborate on reviewing procedures and Squarewise articles. For payment plans, CS will focus solely on information collection and transfer details through the escalation channels for Loan Ops to process the offers. This ensures CS only passes information via the CS Escalation form, while Banking Loan Ops handles all subsequent communication via email. For hold rate reliefs, CS can specify the duration of the relief option. 
 The Squarewise article will be updated with a reminder about this change for both CS and Banking Ops. 
 Once updated, CS leadership will roll out team syncs for change management and refresher to ensure advocates are following the amended process. This streamlined approach applies specifically to hold rate reliefs, as they are straightforward, whereas payment plans require more nuanced negotiation. 
 Looking ahead, CS and Loan Ops may explore digital information collection through Salesforce or SSP rather than verbal communication, allowing Loan Ops to review submissions before sending out or applying relief offers.
Action Plan Validation Steps: Validate review has been completed and changes updated and communicated to the teams
Action Plan Linked To: Issue Management | Issue Management Program | ID 1043021

Action Plan Name: Update to the communication process from Customer Service to Loan Operations
Issue Source: Second Line of Defense (2LoD) Review of First Line of Defense (1LoD) Activities
Action Plan Description:
When Sellers indicate interest in a payment plan, CS will gather comprehensive information including the monthly amount they can afford and their preferred start date. However, CS must clarify that these terms are not guaranteed and remain subject to negotiation with Square. 
 

 A detailed list of questions will be provided in the Squarewise article to guide CS through the information-gathering process. Loan Ops will only offer payment amounts equal to or less than the minimum amount specified in the loan agreement, with minimum payments scheduled every 60 days. This approach ensures fair and consistent outcomes for Sellers. As noted above, the Squarewise articles for CS will be updated, and the Loan Ops SOP will be reviewed and revised accordingly.
Action Plan Validation Steps: Confirm new process has been updated in the SOPS for both CS and LO
Action Plan Linked To: Issue Management | Issue Management Program | ID 1043024

Action Plan Name: Refresher training and review for refunds where a debit has occured
Issue Source: Second Line of Defense (2LoD) Review of First Line of Defense (1LoD) Activities
Action Plan Description:
Re-training and reminders will be implemented for Banking Loan Ops regarding hardship payment plans below the minimum payment amount. 
 

 During the payment plan negotiation stage, a minimum payment delay must be applied to the account for 14 days to prevent debits from being processed. It's important to note that if Sellers request this on the day of the debit, there is no guarantee that applying a minimum payment delay will stop the debit from occurring. Loan Ops will review, if refunds can be provided in certain situations where a debit has occured. If yes, the relevant SOP will be updated. 
 

 Loan Ops will also be reminded to actively monitor both their individual Salesforce queues and the AU Loan Ops Cases queue to ensure responses are being provided to customers.
Action Plan Validation Steps: Confirm training has occurred and review of potential refunds
Action Plan Linked To: Issue Management | Issue Management Program | ID 1043027

Action Plan Name: Exploration of self -serve payment plans and review and updating of macros
Issue Source: Second Line of Defense (2LoD) Review of First Line of Defense (1LoD) Activities
Action Plan Description:
Self-serve payment plans are currently being explored for international markets, including Australia. This feature should significantly reduce the volume of inquiries directed to CS and Loan Ops, as Sellers will be able to set up payment plans independently through their Square Dashboard. While the target rollout is 2026, there is potential for delays. 
 

 Regarding payment plan macros, we will update them to extend the negotiation window for Sellers from 3 days to 14 days (or 10 business days), providing adequate time for them to respond to emails.
 

 A review will also be undertaken to update outdated macros and/or deprecate any macros which are no longer required

Action Plan Name: Management will update SLA documentation, conduct transaction monitoring procedure reviews, perform SLA monitoring and training for transaction monitoring agents on revised requirements.
Action Plan Description:
1. Management will formally confirm and document that the Capacity Model Operations Assumptions – October 2025 document is no longer used as an authoritative source for Transaction Monitoring SLAs.  - Remove references to retired or non-applicable documentation
 - Ensure procedural accuracy and consistency across referenced materials

Action Plan Name: Secure Current PII Storage and Implement Controls
Action Plan Description:
Mitigate immediate risks associated with PII stored in unsecured systems until a long-term solution is implemented.
 -Restrict access to current Google Sheets doc.
 -Remove nonessential personnel from access lists.
 -Implement access permissions (view only mode)
Action Plan Validation Steps:
Access Review Logs
 -Confirm that non-essential personnel have been removed from Google Sheets' access lists.
 -Verify the use of role-appropriate permissions (e.g., "view-only" where applicable).
 -Conduct regular reviews of Google Sheets’ access logs to identify unauthorized or unusual activities.
 

 Incident Reporting Review
 -Confirm that an interim incident reporting mechanism is in place, and that any PII incidents are addressed.
Action Plan Linked To: Issue Management | Issue Management Program | ID 1020518
Closure Request Justification:
All steps outlined in Action Plan 1 to mitigate immediate risks associated with PII stored in unsecured systems have been successfully completed.  
 

 1. Non-essential personnel have been removed from Google Sheets' access lists, ensuring access is restricted to only those who require it. 
 2. Role-appropriate permissions have been verified and implemented to minimize unnecessary data modifications. 
 3. Regular (monthly) reviews ofGoogle Sheets’ access logs are now in place to promptly identify any unauthorized or unusual activities. A monthly access audit guide has been created detailing steps for these reviews. 
 4. Finally, an incident reporting mechanism (go/incident) has been confirmed, allowing for immediate reporting and addressing of any PII-related incidents should they occur.
Closure Validation Justification: All required steps have been taken by the Estates Management Team.

Action Plan Description:
The Uncle Sam system–which cross-checks the identity of borrowers (Borrow and Retro) against the Department of Defense’s internal database to ensure the company charges correct MLA-directed interest rates–experienced a temporary outage. This unknown outage resulted in borrowers’ identities, in relation to military service, to return as “unknown.” Therefore, those borrowers’ loans ultimately received MLA-mandated interest rates/set-up fees, which are lower than standard rates/fees. This is due to standard protocol where the system defaults to applying the lower, mandated rate if the system is not able to access the DoD’s database or is identified as “unknown.” This is to ensure that the MLA is not violated. No batch processing issue monitoring exists for Uncle Sam.
 

 The issue was discovered during a standard review of the loan portfolio where a discrepancy was noted between the number of underwritten loans and projected revenue.
 

 Because of the outage, the system correctly followed standard procedure by defaulting to charge the reduced rate/fee if the system was unable to access Uncle Sam information; this is done as a safeguard to prevent MLA violations.
 

 All impacted loans were.identified for any other discrepancies. None were found, and in the name of preventing possible consumer harm, the loans were allowed to be repaid as underwritten and the loss of revenue written off.
Action Plan Validation Steps: Correction to 'Uncle Sam' was confirmed and validated
Action Plan Linked To: Issue Management | Issue Management Program | ID 1063788
Closure Request Justification: Fix identified, corrected and uplifted to system
